Two new feature flags (`csg-pure` alongside `csg`) plus the existing reveal-all fallback means three meaningful build matrices to keep green in CI. The repo just got back to green-on-main under `-D warnings` (commit d7454a8); doubling the dispatch surface re-introduces matrix complexity right after stabilising it.
verdicts.suggested_mitigation: Make manifold retirement explicit and time-boxed: ship Tier A behind a feature flag, mine the corpus for the actual host-extrusion/cutter-extrusion hit-rate at the IfcRelVoidsElement level (open question #2 — answer it BEFORE building Tier A, not after), and only commit to Tier B (csgrs) if the residual after Tier A is large enough that reveal-all fallback is unacceptable. If Tier A catches >95% as hypothesised, skip csgrs entirely and let Tier B residuals fall through to the existing Outcome::Fallback — that retires manifold AND avoids adopting a second CSG kernel, which is the maintenance-cost-optimal path. Also: use i_overlay's i32/i64 fixed-point API from day one (scale by 1000 — buildings comfortably fit in i32 mm at city scale) to avoid the f32-snap-tolerance landmine becoming a v2 rewrite. Defer Tier C entirely on first ship; for clipped-extrusion + cross-product cases, return Outcome::Fallback until corpus evidence shows it matters.
verdicts.blocker: None blocking — net maintenance cost is plausibly lower than manifold IF the plan retires manifold and skips csgrs. The design as written keeps manifold AND adds csgrs AND adds parametric synthesis, which is strictly more debt; the mitigation above is what makes the lens verdict 'not refuted'.

category.label: General brep × brep / unsupported shapes
category.description: The <1% long tail. Question: typed Unsupported error, or keep manifold behind a feature flag for these alone?

design.shape_category: general-fallback (arbitrary brep × brep / unsupported cutter shapes / non-manifold input / IfcBooleanResult UNION & INTERSECTION / IfcAdvancedBrep approximated / IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid / IfcSweptDiskSolid)
design.primary_algorithm:
Two-tier strategy. The category is heterogeneous, so the right answer is structural — partition on whether a pure-Rust kernel can plausibly cover the case, then assign each subgroup to a tier.

TIER A — REDUCIBLE TO ALREADY-PURE-RUST (no new kernel):
A1. IfcBooleanResult with UNION/INTERSECTION operator. Today `boolean.rs::boolean_result` ignores the operator field and always retags as first/second_operand, which makes the cut_openings partitioner treat second-operand as a cutter even when the author meant UNION or INTERSECTION. Detect this in boolean.rs: read fields[0] (the operator enum), and if it is `.UNION.` or `.INTERSECTION.`, tag both operands with neutral tags (`union_lhs|`, `union_rhs|`, or `intersection_lhs|`, `intersection_rhs|`). cut_openings.rs::is_cutter then returns false for these tags → both operands emit reveal-all. This is principled: reveal-all is correct for non-DIFFERENCE booleans because the consumer can recombine the operands knowing the structural role. No CSG needed.
A2. IfcAdvancedBrep with curved bounding surfaces. Already approximated by faceted outer polyloop in `brep.rs` (tagged `advanced_brep_approx`). Pass through as a host; if it appears as a cutter, that's covered by the brep×brep mesh path (tier B). No new code.
A3. IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered. Currently unhandled fragment. Build a pure-Rust handler in `mesh/extrusion.rs::extrude_tapered`: same start-profile earcut as IfcExtrudedAreaSolid, plus end-profile earcut at the swept distance, plus N×side quads connecting corresponding profile vertices (linear loft). Once meshed, it slots into the existing extrusion+brep cutter pathway. ~120 LOC.
A4. IfcBoxedHalfSpace. Currently unhandled. It's a subtype of IfcHalfSpaceSolid with an explicit AABB. Materialise as a clipped slab against the box (intersect the half-space's infinite slab with the box AABB → finite poly extrusion), then route through the existing `halfspace_clip::clip_by_plane` for the planar cut. The AABB constraint is decorative for our cut-openings purpose: the plane defines what gets removed, the box only constrains where. ~80 LOC.
A5. IfcSweptDiskSolid as a CUTTER. Real-world evidence: zero observed cases (it appears as host product geometry for pipes, never as a cut subtractor). Handle as a host via a new sweep handler in `mesh/swept_disk.rs` (~250 LOC: sample the directrix polycurve at fixed angular resolution, emit a tube of N×M triangles around it, cap the ends). Once meshed, it's a closed solid and can be a cutter via the kernel chosen for tier B if anyone ever authors it as one.
A6. IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid. Same approach as A5 — emit a host mesh by sweeping the 2D profile along the directrix surface curve. ~200 LOC. As a CUTTER: <1% (probably <0.1%) real-world frequency. Route through tier B.

TIER B — STRUCTURALLY 3D-CSG-SHAPED (the irreducible core):
The genuine "long tail" reduces to: closed manifold mesh A minus closed manifold mesh B, where neither side is decomposable into planar half-space clips or axis-aligned-extrusion-vs-axis-aligned-extrusion. Examples: faceted brep wall × extruded opening (Tekla precast with chamfers), IfcPolygonalFaceSet wall × non-axis-aligned extruded opening, faceted brep × faceted brep (rare but exists in some ArchiCAD complex roofs). This is the structural domain of 3D Boolean: BSP-based or mesh-arrangement-based or Nef-polyhedral. There is NO geometric reduction that collapses it into a 2D problem.

PRIMARY RECOMMENDATION FOR TIER B: build a pure-Rust BSP CSG kernel based on the well-trodden CSG.js algorithm (Evan Wallace 2011), now embodied in `csgrs` v0.20.1 (pure Rust, 231★, MIT, depends on nalgebra+parry3d which we already pull in via the geom feature). Algorithm:

  1. Convert each input (host, each cutter) from our flat [x,y,z,...]+[u32] buffers into `csgrs::Polygon<S>` form (one Polygon per triangle, carrying the plane equation and per-vertex normal; we set normals to face normals since we don't carry per-vertex normals).
  2. Validate manifold-ness with a closed-edge check (every directed edge must have an opposite mate). On failure, fast-fall to typed UnsupportedNonManifold error (NOT silent reveal-all — surface it).
  3. Call `csgrs::CSG::difference(&host, &cutter)` — this builds two BSP trees, clips the host's polygons against the cutter's BSP (keeping outside), clips the cutter's polygons against the host's BSP (keeping inside), inverts the kept cutter polygons, and merges. The CSG.js algorithm is well-understood, 200 LOC of math.
  4. For multiple cutters, do `union` of cutters into a single CSG, then a single difference. This matches manifold's `batch_difference` semantics (single CSG tree vs N pairwise) and avoids cumulative tolerance drift.
  5. Convert resulting polygons back to triangle list: each `Polygon` has 3+ vertices, earcut via existing `earcutr` for any 4+vertex polys.

ALTERNATIVE / FALLBACK for tier B (if csgrs proves unreliable on real IFC input): wrap the C++ Manifold library behind the existing `csg` feature flag, keep it default-on for desktop/CI but expose a `pure-rust-only` cargo feature for environments that cannot ship a C++ dep (WASM, statically-linked wheels for unusual platforms, the linux-aarch64 GH#28 case). The current default already enables `csg` so this is a soft change: just make the feature truly optional with a typed `Unsupported::RequiresCsgFeature` error when off.

DISPATCH LOGIC in cut_openings.rs::apply:
- After half-space clips run (current path, pure-rust already), partition solid_cutters by "is the host+cutter pair axis-aligned-extrusion × axis-aligned-extrusion?" — if YES route to the (future) pure-rust extrusion×extrusion kernel from the dominant case. If NO, this is tier B → call `general_csg::difference(host, cutters)` which is csgrs-backed.
- On csgrs failure: surface `Outcome::TypedUnsupported(reason)` (new variant) rather than silent `Outcome::Fallback`. The reveal-all stance still applies (mesh untouched) but the consumer now gets a categorised reason: NonManifoldInput, SelfIntersectingCutter, CoplanarFaceDegeneracy, CsgKernelInternalError. Aggregate counts per reason in `CutOpeningsStats` so AGENTS.md consumers can act.

WHY NOT MANIFOLD-BEHIND-FEATURE-FLAG AS PRIMARY: it's already there and only catches a fraction of the long tail (GH#39, GH#28/1714 showed manifold's tolerance handling is hostile to bbox-derived bounding meshes; non-manifold Tekla breps were already documented as Fallback). Keeping it behind a flag for "these alone" misses the point: the tier-B cases that need a real 3D CSG kernel deserve a kernel the user can rely on without a C++ toolchain. csgrs is the pure-Rust answer, with manifold kept as an OPTIONAL second engine for cases where csgrs fails (consumer toggles a feature to fallback-fallback to manifold).

WHY NOT TYPED-UNSUPPORTED-ERROR-FOR-ALL: ~few-% of files (Tekla precast, ArchiCAD complex roofs) genuinely need brep×brep Boolean. Returning Unsupported for them would degrade ifcfast on a non-negligible market segment and force users back to ifcopenshell for those files — defeating the "agent-first parser that does the right thing" positioning.
prerequisites: Pure-Rust general CSG kernel module `crates/core/src/geom/csg_pure.rs` wrapping `csgrs` (add dep `csgrs = "0.20"`). Buffer adapter to/from our flat [f32;N]+[u32;M] form. Manifold-ness validator (closed-edge check). ~250 LOC + 200 LOC tests.

Typed Unsupported error variant on Outcome enum: `enum Outcome { Cut, Passthrough, Fallback, Unsupported(UnsupportedReason) }` with UnsupportedReason carrying NonManifoldInput / SelfIntersectingCutter / CoplanarFaceDegeneracy / CsgKernelInternalError / CurvedSurfaceApproximated / UnhandledCutterEntity{type_name}. ~60 LOC across cut_openings.rs and lib.rs stats. Backwards-compat: keep Fallback as a catch-all, Unsupported is a refinement.

Reason-aggregated stats: extend CutOpeningsStats with `pub by_unsupported: HashMap<UnsupportedReason, usize>` and surface in lib.rs Cut/CutCrossProduct accumulators (3 call sites already exist). ~40 LOC.
IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered handler `mesh/extrusion.rs::extrude_tapered` (linear loft start→end profile, side quads, two earcut caps). ~120 LOC + 80 LOC tests.

IfcBoxedHalfSpace handler in `mesh/boolean.rs::boxed_halfspace` (clip the half-space slab against the explicit AABB → finite polygon extrusion → existing halfspace_clip route). ~80 LOC + 40 LOC tests.
IfcSweptDiskSolid handler `mesh/swept_disk.rs` (sample directrix polycurve, emit N-segment tube, two end caps). ~250 LOC + 150 LOC tests.
IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid handler `mesh/swept_area.rs` (sample directrix on surface, sweep 2D profile along it). ~200 LOC + 100 LOC tests.

IfcBooleanResult operator-aware tagging in `mesh/boolean.rs::boolean_result`: read fields[0], emit `union_*` / `intersection_*` / current `boolean_*_operand` tags accordingly. ~30 LOC. is_cutter() update in cut_openings.rs to only match `boolean_second_operand`. ~5 LOC.

Dispatch refactor in cut_openings.rs::apply: introduce `route_cutter(host_kind, cutter_kind) -> CsgPath` enum (Halfspace / ExtrusionMinusExtrusion / GeneralCsg). Plumb host_kind / cutter_kind from segment source tags. Currently the partitioner only knows host vs cutter — needs an extra discriminator from the source-tag chain. ~120 LOC.
Optional manifold-as-secondary-fallback: when csgrs returns Err and the `csg` feature is enabled, retry through `geom::csg::subtract_many` before declaring Unsupported. ~30 LOC.

Test fixtures: a synthetic Tekla-style closed faceted brep with a chamfered corner minus a rectangular opening; a non-manifold open-shell wall minus an extrusion (must yield typed Unsupported(NonManifoldInput), not silent Fallback); a UNION boolean (must yield reveal-all, both operands visible).

AGENTS.md update documenting: new Unsupported reason taxonomy, csgrs in the pure-rust toolchain, the dispatch tree, and which IFC entities are now handled at host vs cutter position (tapered, boxed halfspace, swept disk, swept area).
edge_cases: Faceted brep with a single T-junction on an edge (Tekla precast quirk). csgrs's BSP partition treats the T as a non-manifold edge → returns garbage or panics. Mitigation: pre-validate closed-edge with HashMap<(u32,u32),i32> sign accumulator; require every undirected edge to appear with sign sum 0 (one +1, one −1). On failure → Unsupported(NonManifoldInput) with the offending edge surfaced in stats.

Self-intersecting cutter (ArchiCAD complex opening with a hairpin profile). csgrs BSP descends into a degenerate split that never terminates well. Mitigation: pre-check using parry3d's TriMesh intersection on the cutter against itself (sample 5% of triangles, fail fast). Or accept csgrs's failure mode and route to Unsupported. Defer decision pending csgrs behaviour test.

Coplanar face between host and cutter (a wall whose inner face is exactly the back face of the recess opening — Revit emits this for blind recesses). CSG.js / csgrs BSP has a known degenerate: coplanar polygons need a `COPLANAR_FRONT` / `COPLANAR_BACK` classifier that depends on dot(face_normal, plane_normal) sign. csgrs implements this but the user must respect winding consistency. We do; verify with a regression test.

Wall tilted 30° from vertical with a non-vertical opening (sloped curtain wall with a window). The host is no longer axis-aligned; in our local-rebased frame the wall extrusion direction is some arbitrary unit vector, opening's extrusion direction another. The pure-rust BSP doesn't care — it works in arbitrary 3D — but the dispatch step that decides 'is this extrusion×extrusion?' must NOT assume Z-up. The dispatch test should be on whether the cutter's source tag chain shows `extrusion` for both sides, not on world-frame axis alignment.

Opening that extends beyond the wall on both sides (a through-pipe modelled as a long extrusion). csgrs handles this exactly — it computes the intersection of the cutter's volume with the host volume and removes only that intersection. Validates against the existing manifold test `subtract_boundary_straddling_opening_returns_net_solid` (line 266 csg.rs).

Cutter exactly tangent to host face (opening flush with wall back face, depth = wall thickness). The 'exit' face of the opening sits exactly on the wall's back face. Coplanar degeneracy — must produce a closed result with NO sliver triangles on the back face. csgrs handles this via its coplanar classifier; tolerance dial may need tuning. Test fixture required.

Nested halfspace clip INSIDE the host extrusion BEFORE the opening cut (sloped wall with door). Current flow: in-rep apply (halfspace_clip on the wall) runs first; then the cross-product opening folds via the new general kernel on the already-clipped wall. The clipped wall is a closed manifold (halfspace_clip caps the cut), so the second-stage CSG sees a valid input. Verify: the existing GH#39 deep-stack test plus an added opening must still produce correct volume.

IfcBooleanResult with UNION operator inside an IfcBooleanClippingResult tree (rare but legal). After A1 fix, the UNION operands tag as `union_lhs`/`union_rhs` even inside a clipping wrapper, so `is_cutter` short-circuits to false for both. The clipping wrapper still adds its own `boolean_first_operand`/`boolean_second_operand` outer tag — chain becomes `boolean_first_operand|union_lhs|extrusion` — `any(link == "boolean_second_operand")` correctly says 'this is host'. UNION still acts as merge in reveal-all (both operands visible) which is the right behaviour because we did not subtract anything.

IfcAdvancedBrep cutter with a curved bounding surface (e.g. an IfcCylindricalSurface as the cutter's outer shell). We approximate to outer polyloop, so the cylindrical face becomes a planar approximation. CSG difference produces a wrong (flat-faced) hole. We must surface this loudly: tag the result mesh segment as `cut_openings|curved_cutter_approximated` so consumers can warn. Stats: bump `Unsupported(CurvedSurfaceApproximated)` count but DO emit the approximate cut (it's better than reveal-all for visualisation, worse than exact for QTO).

Far-from-origin precision. Mesh anchors get vertices into local frame <10m apart, so f32 is fine for csgrs's f64-promoted internals. But the BSP recursion depth at coplanar near-miss can amplify round-off. Test: do the existing 'sannergata 13-deep' case still terminates within reasonable depth (csgrs accepts a max-depth parameter; default 100 should suffice).

Empty cutter (zero-triangle opening — e.g. an IfcOpeningElement with no geometry due to upstream extraction failure). csgrs treats empty as identity. Match current manifold behaviour: skip the empty cutter, no error.

Empty result (host fully contained in cutter — over-large opening). csgrs returns empty CSG. Same handling as current `subtract_many` empty result path in `cut_openings.rs::apply` line 131-134: the host is emptied, mesh.indices becomes empty, segment is `cut_openings` with index_count=0. Caller decides whether that's an error or a 'door is the whole wall' user error.

Mixed half-space + general CSG cutters on one host (Sannergata wall with 13 halfspaces + 2 cross-product door openings). Sequence: half-space clips first (already pure-rust, GH#39 fix), then doors via csgrs. The intermediate mesh between the two passes is the cap-stitched output of halfspace_clip — verify it's manifold-clean enough for csgrs (closed-edge check on the intermediate would catch any sliver from the cap triangulator).

IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid as host being cut. Once A6 ships, the host mesh is a sampled sweep. Manifold-ness of a sampled sweep depends on the directrix smoothness — sharp corners in the directrix produce non-manifold T-junctions where adjacent sweep segments don't match exactly. Cap with a topology cleanup pass (weld coincident vertices within 1e-3 mm) before handing to csgrs.
design.loc_estimate: ~1.5k LOC core (csg_pure adapter + UnsupportedReason taxonomy + dispatch refactor + four new tessellation handlers — tapered, boxed-halfspace, swept-disk, swept-area), ~800 LOC tests, plus ~150 LOC AGENTS.md/doc updates. Total ≈ 2.5k LOC. The csgrs dependency itself adds ~5k LOC compile cost but zero authoring cost.
open_questions: csgrs production-readiness on real IFC input. Stars/commits look healthy (231★, 998 commits, v0.20.1) but pre-1.0; the BSP CSG.js algorithm itself is well-understood, but the question is whether the crate's tolerance handling, coplanar classifier, and panic-safety hold up on Tekla/ArchiCAD bad-input. Suggested probe: build a benchmark of 5–10 known-difficult files (Sannergata, Duplex, a Tekla precast pack) and run csgrs subtract_many on every IfcRelVoidsElement pair, count crashes / wrong-volume results. If failure rate >5%, reconsider — possibilities include forking csgrs, contributing fixes, or accepting tier-B Unsupported and routing to manifold-fallback.

Should we expose csgrs as the SOLE Tier-B engine and drop manifold entirely (simplifies the build, removes the C++ dep, closes GH#28 linux-aarch64), or keep manifold as an opt-in `csg-manifold-fallback` feature for the cases csgrs can't handle? Recommendation pending the probe above. If csgrs ≥95% match on benchmark, drop manifold. Else keep both.

How to expose the new Unsupported reason taxonomy through PyO3 / parquet / CLI? Today `CutOpeningsStats` is three ints surfaced via lib.rs accumulators; adding a HashMap<Enum, usize> needs serialisation across the FFI boundary. Simplest: per-reason counter fields on the stats struct (one int per known reason); 5–7 fields, no map. Confirm with the AGENTS.md consumer contract.

Should IfcSweptDiskSolid and IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid handlers be in scope for the cut-openings work, or spun off as their own GH issues? Real-world frequency as a cutter is essentially zero, so they're more of a host-shape concern; argues for spinning them off, but they affect the dispatcher (currently they'd be `Unsupported(UnhandledCutterEntity)`). Suggest: ship the dispatcher with named Unsupported variants for these now, ship the handlers as follow-ons (GH issues).

Coplanar tolerance for csgrs. The current halfspace_clip uses 1e-3 mm (below building tolerance). csgrs default may be different — possibly 1e-6 in unit-scaled space. We're in mm, so csgrs's default tolerance in our units is 1e-6 mm which is way below f32 noise. Verify csgrs accepts a tolerance configuration parameter; if not, may need to fork or wrap. Test required.

BSP recursion depth on pathological inputs (Sannergata-class 13-deep clip already exists as a halfspace path; pure CSG depth at the brep×brep level could exceed 100 on Tekla compound voids). csgrs's CSG.js heritage has a known O(N²) worst-case on coplanar-heavy input. Investigate: does csgrs offer iterative or bounded-depth modes? If not, may need fuzzy-bools-style mesh-arrangement as the backstop (more code, more robust but slower).

Do we need to handle IfcBooleanResult with INTERSECTION operator beyond just tagging it neutrally? Use case: a wall that's defined as `roof_volume ∩ wall_extrusion` (a wall under a sloped roof). Reveal-all surfaces both volumes but the consumer wouldn't know they should intersect. Could surface this via a new segment source `intersection_lhs|...` and document in AGENTS.md, or actually compute the intersection via csgrs::intersect. Defer to the consumer-feedback channel.

Performance budget. The current manifold path costs ~50 ms/wall on Sannergata at peak. csgrs is reportedly slower (no published benchmarks vs manifold). If it's 5× slower, the parallel tessellation pass may need to scale csgrs workers separately to keep walltime within target. Need a perf probe on a known-large file (Sannergata 379 walls).

verdicts.shape_category: general-fallback (arbitrary brep × brep / unsupported cutter shapes / non-manifold input / IfcBooleanResult UNION & INTERSECTION / IfcAdvancedBrep approximated / IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid / IfcSweptDiskSolid)
concerns: INTERSECTION operator under A1 is silently miscoded as reveal-all (union of operands), which materially falsifies QTO. A wall defined as wall_extrusion intersection roof_volume would surface both volumes as solid mesh — the consumer sees ~2x the true material volume. The design author acknowledges this in open_questions but still proposes the wrong behaviour as the shipped default. INTERSECTION must either be computed exactly (csgrs::intersect) or fail loudly via Unsupported(IntersectionNotImplemented), never silently reveal-all.

A1's tag-based UNION/INTERSECTION short-circuit is structurally broken under nesting. mesh::boolean::retag (lines 297–308) preserves the INNERMOST role (`role.unwrap_or(new_role)`). For UNION(DIFFERENCE(A,B), C), the inner DIFFERENCE still stamps B with the innermost `boolean_second_operand` link before the outer UNION wraps it. cut_openings::is_cutter scans the chain with `.any(== "boolean_second_operand")` and still fires on B — UNION's no-cut semantic is NOT preserved. To make A1 correct, the design needs an outer-overrides-inner invalidation pass, not just an extra inner check. The proposed ~5 LOC update to is_cutter does not address this.

Mixed pipeline (half-space clips → csgrs) is a correctness regression on the GH#39 Sannergata 13-deep case. halfspace_clip's cap earcut emits sliver triangles and T-junctions at the cap/extrusion-side boundary; the design's pre-validation (HashMap<(u32,u32),i32> sign-sum closed-edge check) will reject these as NonManifoldInput. Files that today produce Outcome::Cut would regress to Outcome::Unsupported(NonManifoldInput). The closed-edge check needs a vertex-weld tolerance plus T-junction healing pass before it can sit in front of csgrs.

Worked example (1m×0.25m×3m wall − 0.9m×0.3m×2.1m door, expected 0.2775 m³) cannot be verified end-to-end from the design — it depends on csgrs's coplanar tolerance and BSP coplanar classifier behaviour, both flagged as `Test fixture required` / `verify with a regression test` in the design itself. The design ships unverified correctness on the single most common pattern (Revit recess flush with back face = coplanar exit) the long-tail kernel must handle. The recommendation is medium-confidence on shape that needs evidence-based confidence.

Winding normalization is assumed but not in the spec. csgrs needs consistent CCW outward winding for its plane-equation Polygon constructor; faceted brep and IfcAdvancedBrep imports do NOT enforce CCW (only indexed_curve does, per v0.4.30 GH#48 note). A CW-wound host fed to csgrs::difference computes the complement difference (cutter minus host, then inverted) — silently producing the wrong solid. The design must add a winding-normalization pre-pass that recomputes face orientations from a flood-fill outward-vote, or csgrs will hand back inside-out meshes that look plausible to a glb viewer but are catastrophically wrong for clash and QTO.

Far-from-origin (Sannergata-class) f32 input feeds csgrs's BSP. The opening anchor difference `(opening.mesh_anchor - host.mesh_anchor) as f32` in CrossProductCut::flush truncates f64 to f32 BEFORE csgrs sees it. At ~1e6 m project coordinates, even after local rebase to ~10 m anchors, accumulated f32 round-off of vertices is ~1e-6 m × 10 = ~1e-5 m. csgrs may promote to f64 internally but the input near-coplanar faces are already perturbed — the BSP coplanar classifier sees them as off-plane and produces sliver triangles in the output. Volume agrees to ~1 ppm; the mesh is no longer suitable for downstream clash (where 1mm threshold is meaningful) or QTO surface-area extraction. The design does not propose a higher-precision (f64) buffer pass for the CSG step.

IfcAdvancedBrep curved-cutter approximation (edge_case 9) is acknowledged as producing a 'wrong (flat-faced) hole' but the design still emits the approximate cut alongside an Unsupported(CurvedSurfaceApproximated) counter. The mesh is silently incorrect: a 100mm-radius cylindrical column drilling through a wall ends up as a 4-sided prism hole (or however many side faces the IfcAdvancedBrep face approximation produced). For QTO this is a 21% volume error. The reveal-all stance is explicitly violated here (we DID alter the host mesh) without surfacing the alteration in the segment tag visible to a glb consumer — only via a stats counter that the viewer ignores.

Empty-result handling for over-large opening (cutter contains host) — the design defers to existing line 131-134 behaviour where `host.indices` becomes empty and the segment tag is `cut_openings` with index_count=0. But the design also adds `Outcome::Unsupported(...)` variants. The empty-result case is NOT an error — it is a legitimate solution. The design does not specify what Outcome variant fires when csgrs::difference returns empty: is it Cut (with empty mesh) or Unsupported (because nothing visible came out)? Inconsistent handling here will break the substrate writer's stats reporting.
verdicts.blocker: INTERSECTION-as-reveal-all is a silent semantic falsehood that the design ships as the default for a category in scope, AND the UNION tag-propagation through retag is structurally broken under nesting. Either alone would refute the design at the correctness lens; together they confirm the design does not survive the operator-aware partitioning it relies on. The csgrs coplanar/winding/precision unknowns compound the issue — the design cannot demonstrate correctness on the worked Revit-recess example without test evidence that does not exist.
verdicts.suggested_mitigation: Three changes to make the design survive the correctness lens. (1) Make INTERSECTION a typed Unsupported(IntersectionNotImplemented) for v1; defer the csgrs::intersect path to a follow-on with its own benchmark — do NOT silently reveal-all. UNION can ship as reveal-all but only after fixing retag to let an OUTER union tag invalidate an inner boolean_second_operand link (introduce a separate `composite_role` field on MeshSegment that records the OUTERMOST composite role, then is_cutter checks: chain contains `boolean_second_operand` AND outermost composite_role is DIFFERENCE). (2) Add a winding-normalization pre-pass at the csgrs adapter boundary: flood-fill face adjacency, vote on outward orientation by computing the signed volume contribution, flip any face whose contribution disagrees with the majority. Mandatory before csgrs sees the buffer. (3) Promote the csgrs input buffers to f64 at the adapter (translate by host_anchor at f64, build Polygon at f64, demote output to f32 only for the gltf write). This sidesteps the coplanar-jitter regression on Sannergata-class far-origin files. ALSO: pre-stage csgrs behind a benchmark gate before any release — the open_questions list a 5–10 file probe; that probe must run BEFORE merging the dispatch refactor, not after, because the dispatch refactor is the irreversible part of the design.

verdicts.shape_category: general-fallback (arbitrary brep × brep / unsupported cutter shapes / non-manifold input / IfcBooleanResult UNION & INTERSECTION / IfcAdvancedBrep approximated / IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid / IfcSweptDiskSolid)
concerns: Coplanar-face handling is hand-waved. CSG.js/csgrs's classifier needs a numerical EPSILON (default ~1e-5 in the original Wallace code) interpreted in source units. The design notes 'we are in mm, so csgrs's default tolerance in our units is 1e-6 mm which is way below f32 noise', BUT ifcfast emits vertices in source units, which can be metres, mm, feet, or inches per file (see Substrate unit_scale memory). A 1e-5 EPSILON in METRES is 10 microns — fine; in MM it's 1e-5 mm — far below f32 noise, classifier flips back and forth on the same coplanar polygon, producing sliver triangles or dropped faces. The 'verify csgrs accepts a tolerance configuration' is listed as an OPEN QUESTION, not a prerequisite — the design ships with a known-unsafe default for the most common degeneracy in real IFC data (Revit blind recesses with back-face-flush openings). This is the single most frequent degeneracy and it has no concrete mitigation.

Self-intersecting cutter handling is conditional and deferred. ArchiCAD and Revit BOTH emit self-intersecting cutters in practice (hairpin profiles, bowtie sweeps from curved walls). The mitigation reads 'Or accept csgrs's failure mode and route to Unsupported. Defer decision pending csgrs behaviour test.' BSP-based CSG on self-intersecting input does not gracefully fail — it either produces wrong volumes silently (a hole appears where none was authored, or vice-versa) or descends into pathological recursion. There is NO pre-validation step in the prerequisites list for self-intersection on the cutter (only closed-edge manifold check, which a bowtie passes). The closed-edge check catches non-manifoldness, not self-intersection — these are orthogonal failure modes. A self-intersecting closed manifold IS valid topology but invalid geometry, and csgrs will silently produce garbage.

Far-from-origin precision claim is wrong in detail. The design says 'Mesh anchors get vertices into local frame <10m apart, so f32 is fine for csgrs's f64-promoted internals'. But (a) csgrs is NOT documented as f64-promoted internally — that was manifold's behaviour, not csgrs's; csgrs uses nalgebra Real which is generic but defaults to f64 in their CSG type, however the BSP plane equations are computed from f32 inputs and round-off happens at the INPUT-conversion step, not internally; (b) the local-rebase invariant (vertices <10m apart) applies to the mesh-anchor representation but the BSP plane equation `ax+by+cz+d=0` carries `d` as `dot(normal, point_on_plane)`, and `d` is in the same magnitude as the vertex coords AFTER rebase but the EPSILON test compares `|d_v - d_p|` against an absolute constant. The design does not establish that csgrs scales EPSILON relative to the input bbox. If it doesn't, far-origin-rebased meshes near 0 plus the unit-scale ambiguity above compound the coplanar-classifier instability.

Degenerate-cutter early-catch is missing entirely. The edge_cases list mentions 'Empty cutter (zero-triangle opening)' but NOT (a) zero-area profile cutters (IfcExtrudedAreaSolid with a profile whose three points are colinear — Revit emits this from corrupted families), nor (b) zero-extrude-length cutters (Depth=0 mm openings from Revit family parameter defaults). These appear as cutters with non-empty vertex/index buffers (the tessellator dutifully produces a degenerate prism) but with all triangles having area ≈ 0 or all vertices coplanar in a non-bounding way. csgrs's BSP will build a tree from these and either panic on a zero-normal plane or classify every host polygon as COPLANAR. There is no pre-flight area / volume sanity check on cutters in the prerequisites — only a closed-edge check.

The intermediate-mesh-between-halfspace-and-CSG case is asserted, not validated. Edge case 12 claims 'the intermediate mesh between the two passes is the cap-stitched output of halfspace_clip — verify it's manifold-clean enough for csgrs (closed-edge check on the intermediate would catch any sliver from the cap triangulator).' This is a TODO, not a mitigation. The cap triangulator in halfspace_clip is known to produce slivers at extreme angles (the 13-deep Sannergata stack is at the edge of its tolerance, per GH#39 history). Slivers ARE non-manifold (T-junctions and near-duplicate vertices) and the closed-edge check on the cap-stitched intermediate WILL fail on real data. The design then routes to Unsupported — meaning Sannergata's worst walls degrade from 'cut correctly via halfspace_clip + manifold' (today) to 'Unsupported(NonManifoldInput)' (proposed). That's a regression on the very file the existing kernel was tuned for.

Coplanar tolerance in the IfcAdvancedBrep approximation path. The brep handler approximates curved surfaces by faceted outer polyloop. When such an approximation is used as a HOST and a planar cutter happens to land near (but not exactly on) one of the facets, you get a band of near-coplanar polygons that the BSP will classify inconsistently — some COPLANAR_FRONT, some COPLANAR_BACK, some SPANNING — producing a striped output. The design acknowledges curved-cutter approximation produces wrong holes but does NOT address curved-HOST × planar-cutter approximation noise, which is the more common case.

Empty-result semantics under tolerance. Edge case 11 ('Empty result (host fully contained in cutter)') says 'csgrs returns empty CSG. Same handling as current subtract_many empty result path'. But with a too-tight EPSILON, csgrs may produce a near-empty result (handful of sliver triangles) rather than truly empty. The current empty-result path checks `mesh.indices.is_empty()`; near-empty falls through as a valid mesh and ships visible-but-meaningless slivers to the viewer. No tolerance-aware emptiness check is specified.
verdicts.blocker: Coplanar-face handling depends on a csgrs EPSILON that is treated as an open question rather than a prerequisite. For the most common real-world degeneracy in this category (Revit blind recess with back-face-flush opening — coplanar host-back-face and cutter-exit-face), the design has no concrete mitigation: it documents the failure mode in edge_cases, defers tolerance verification to open_questions, and ships a kernel whose default EPSILON is provably wrong under at least one of the unit systems ifcfast supports (mm). A regression test 'required' is not a fix.
verdicts.suggested_mitigation: Before adopting csgrs as the primary tier-B engine, gate the work on three concrete prerequisites (not open questions): (1) a unit-aware EPSILON adapter that scales csgrs's coplanar tolerance to `max(file_unit_scale * 1e-4, bbox_diagonal * 1e-7)` and verifies csgrs actually exposes a tolerance parameter (if not, fork-or-replace decision must be made before any code lands, not after); (2) a cutter pre-flight validator covering BOTH non-manifoldness (closed-edge) AND degeneracy (per-triangle area > epsilon, total volume > epsilon, no self-intersection via parry3d TriMesh-self-intersection) — degenerate cutters route to typed Unsupported(DegenerateCutter) without ever touching the BSP; (3) a manifold-cleanup pass between halfspace_clip and the tier-B kernel (vertex-weld within unit-aware tolerance, T-junction split, sliver-triangle collapse) — without this, the cap-stitched intermediate from existing GH#39 fixtures will fail the new closed-edge gate and regress today's working cases. Until those three land, keep manifold as the primary tier-B engine behind the existing feature flag and ship csgrs as an opt-in `csg-pure` alternative — exactly the opposite of the design's current 'csgrs primary, manifold optional fallback' posture. The typed-Unsupported taxonomy and the tier-A handlers (tapered, boxed-halfspace, swept-disk, swept-area, UNION/INTERSECTION tagging) are independently valuable and can land first.

verdicts.shape_category: general-fallback (arbitrary brep × brep / unsupported cutter shapes / non-manifold input / IfcBooleanResult UNION & INTERSECTION / IfcAdvancedBrep approximated / IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid / IfcSweptDiskSolid)
concerns: Algorithmic class regression vs current manifold path. The proposal makes csgrs (BSP-based, CSG.js lineage) the PRIMARY Tier-B engine and demotes manifold to opt-in fallback. Manifold's own positioning vs BSP libraries is 10-100x faster on closed-mesh booleans because BSP construction is O(N^2) worst-case while manifold uses parallel mesh arrangement on contiguous arrays. The proposal does not produce a single measured number for csgrs on real IFC input before committing — open_questions explicitly defers the perf probe.

Per-product cost is materially worse. BSP construction with N triangles allocates O(N log N) Polygon nodes typical and O(N^2) worst-case. Each polygon split clones vertex data into front+back lists. A 200-triangle wall minus a 60-triangle opening yields ~10-20k Vec allocations per cut. Compare manifold: a couple of contiguous arena allocations per operation plus SIMD ops over packed f32x4 lanes. The proposal cites manifold at ~50 ms/wall on Sannergata; even a generous 5x slowdown puts csgrs at 250 ms/wall, and a Sannergata-class coplanar-heavy file pushes toward 20x = 1 s/wall.

Project-scale wall-clock blows past the manifold baseline. For a 10k product / 5k IfcRelVoidsElement project: manifold path at 50 ms/wall = 250 s sequential, ~31 s on T=8 parallel. csgrs at 5x baseline = 156 s on T=8 (5x regression); csgrs at 20x baseline on coplanar-heavy walls = 625 s on T=8 (a 20x regression, ~10 minutes wall-clock). This crosses the threshold from 'a coffee-break wait' to 'unusable in an interactive feedback loop'.

Dispatcher routing inflates tier-B traffic. The dispatch tree routes all non-extrusion-vs-extrusion to GeneralCsg, but the prerequisites also queue IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered, IfcBoxedHalfSpace, IfcSweptDiskSolid, and IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid handlers as the new mesh primitives. Tapered extrusions appear in any sloped wall; swept-disk solids appear in EVERY MEP pipe segment. The 'long tail <1%' framing in the question understates what hits the slow engine once the dispatcher exists. The proposal hand-waves a future 'pure-rust extrusion-vs-extrusion kernel from the dominant case' — that kernel does not exist, so today everything that does not fit halfspace_clip routes to the slow path.

Allocator pressure is unaddressed. BSP polygon-split is the canonical antipattern for the global allocator: deep recursion, many small Vec<Vertex> allocations (3-12 vertices each), no arena reuse across cuts on the same host. With rayon worker threads (the existing parallel tess path), every worker contends on the global heap. The proposal mentions parallelism but does not specify a bumpalo / typed-arena strategy for the csgrs polygon nodes.

Manifold's batch_difference performance characteristic is lost. Today, subtract_many feeds N cutters in a single CSG operation; manifold internally unions cutters once and does one host-vs-cutter pass, amortising fixed cost. csgrs's pattern in the proposal is 'union cutters first then difference' — that's two BSP constructions where manifold did one combined sweep, and csgrs unions are themselves O(N log N) BSP builds. The proposal acknowledges this matches manifold semantics but not the cost: csgrs needs at minimum 2x the BSP work per host.

Compile-time and runtime regressions from the new dep tree. csgrs depends on nalgebra and parry3d (which we already pull via the geom feature for clash) but at versions that may diverge — version unification could pin our existing nalgebra back, or fork the build. 'csgrs adds ~5k LOC compile cost' in the proposal undersells: nalgebra itself is a heavy generic-laden crate that adds substantial cold-build time, and any version conflict forces duplicate compilation. CI wall-clock matters for the ifcfast release flow (push v-tag, CI publishes).

BSP recursion depth on real input is a known cliff. The proposal flags this in open_questions #6 (Sannergata 13-deep clip plus brep-vs-brep coplanar-heavy could exceed depth 100) but resolves it as 'investigate later'. CSG.js's O(N^2) worst case is the documented failure mode of every BSP CSG library on building geometry — and building geometry is exactly the input set. If csgrs has no iterative or bounded-depth mode, the worst-case is not 5x or 20x manifold, it is unbounded or stack-overflow.
verdicts.suggested_mitigation:
Invert the commitment order. Do not adopt csgrs as primary before a hard perf measurement. Concretely: (1) Build a perf probe FIRST — 5-10 representative files (Sannergata 379 walls, Duplex, a Tekla precast pack, an ArchiCAD complex-roof file). Run csgrs::CSG::difference on every IfcRelVoidsElement pair, record per-pair wall-clock and total project wall-clock. Compare against the current manifold path on identical pairs. (2) Set a hard performance gate: csgrs is acceptable as primary only if median slowdown <= 2x and p99 <= 5x manifold on the probe set. (3) If the gate fails — which the BSP literature strongly suggests it will — keep manifold as the primary tier-B engine and use csgrs only as a fallback for manifold-rejected inputs (typed Unsupported::ManifoldRejected -> retry csgrs -> typed Unsupported::CsgKernelInternalError). This preserves manifold's perf for the common case while still solving GH#28's pure-rust-only requirement via a `pure-rust` cargo feature that explicitly accepts the perf trade. (4) For tier-A handlers (tapered extrusion, boxed halfspace, swept disk, swept area), ship them independently as pure-rust mesh primitives — they do not require any tier-B kernel. (5) Mandate a bumpalo arena per worker for csgrs Polygon nodes if it does land — global-allocator BSP across rayon workers will thrash. (6) Set a hard BSP max-depth (e.g., 64) on csgrs and surface depth-exceeded as typed Unsupported(BspDepthExceeded), then fall back to manifold for those specific inputs rather than panic. (7) Drop A5/A6 (IfcSweptDiskSolid and IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid handlers) from this scope entirely — they are host-mesh concerns, not cut-openings concerns, and ship as separate GH issues so the dispatcher does not silently route every pipe segment through tier-B when these eventually exist.</suggested_mitigation>
<parameter name="blocker">Project-scale wall-clock regression. On a 10k-product / 5k-void project, the proposal's primary path (csgrs as Tier-B engine) is, by the published BSP-vs-mesh-arrangement perf literature and manifold's own positioning, 5-20x slower than the current manifold path. That translates to ~2-10 minutes of cut_openings wall-clock at T=8 parallel where the current path completes in ~30 seconds. The proposal commits to this trade before measuring it — its own open_questions defer the perf probe — and does not name a measured threshold at which the design would be reconsidered.</blocker>
</invoke>

verdicts.shape_category: general-fallback (arbitrary brep × brep / unsupported cutter shapes / non-manifold input / IfcBooleanResult UNION & INTERSECTION / IfcAdvancedBrep approximated / IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid / IfcSweptDiskSolid)
concerns: Bus-factor on csgrs is the central undisclosed risk. csgrs v0.20.x is pre-1.0, ~231 stars, and (per the design's own open questions) has no published benchmarks against manifold and unknown tolerance-config surface. The design treats it as a 'pure-Rust answer' but it is in fact a single-maintainer hobby crate ported from a 2011 JavaScript demo (CSG.js). Manifold, by contrast, has Google's geometry-processing community behind it (Emmett Lalish, ex-Microsoft), an active issue tracker, and a published correctness proof. The design swaps a well-validated C++ kernel for a less-validated Rust one and counts that as a maintenance WIN — that's backwards. If csgrs goes stale, WE inherit the BSP CSG implementation, including the O(N^2) coplanar pathology the open questions already flag.

Owning four NEW tessellation handlers forever (tapered, boxed-halfspace, swept-disk, swept-area, ~650 LOC + 370 LOC tests) is real long-term debt that the design rolls into the 'fallback' bucket where it does not belong. None of these four needed brep×brep CSG; they each could ship as independent host-only handlers gated by their own GH issues. Bundling them into the tier-B story inflates the LOC estimate, couples four unrelated review surfaces to one PR, and obscures the actual maintenance question (which kernel for the irreducible 3D CSG core). This is poor maintenance hygiene regardless of which kernel wins.

The dispatch refactor adds a permanent two-engine fork (csgrs primary + manifold opt-in fallback-fallback), which is strictly MORE long-term debt than either kernel alone. Every future bug report now requires reproducing on both engines, every tolerance dial has two values, every CI matrix doubles, every Unsupported reason taxonomy entry needs mapping in two directions. 'Keep manifold as opt-in' sounds cheap on paper; in practice it means we never delete the C++ build path the design claims to escape from. The honest options are (a) commit fully to csgrs and accept the bus-factor risk, or (b) keep manifold as the sole tier-B engine and fix GH#28 via aarch64-specific work — not both.

The UnsupportedReason taxonomy with HashMap<Enum, usize> stats surfaced through PyO3/parquet/CLI is a public-API expansion the design under-costs. Every new reason variant added later becomes a backwards-compat hazard on the parquet schema (cache_schema_version bump), the PyO3 dataclass, the AGENTS.md contract, AND the CLI output format. The design's own open question 5 admits the FFI surface is unresolved. Adding a typed error enum is fine; threading it through five surfaces is debt that compounds with every new IFC quirk we discover — which is exactly what the design claims to be solving.

The 'ongoing tuning as we encounter new IFC quirks' question is answered honestly by the edge-cases section itself: T-junctions, self-intersecting cutters, coplanar degeneracies, sloped walls, tangent cutters, nested halfspace+CSG, curved-surface approximation, far-from-origin precision, BSP recursion depth, empty inputs, empty results, mixed cutters, sampled-sweep manifoldness. That is 13 tuning axes BEFORE shipping. The design claims this is 'lower-debt long-term' than manifold, but manifold's debt is a one-time build-system problem (C++ toolchain, cross-compile). The csgrs debt is a recurring per-file-quirk problem that lives in our code. Build debt is amortisable; algorithmic-correctness debt is not.

The pre-validation manifold-ness check (closed-edge HashMap accumulator) is presented as ~250 LOC of adapter code, but real-world Tekla/ArchiCAD breps will fail this check at non-trivial rates, routing them to Unsupported and degrading ifcfast on exactly the 'non-negligible market segment' the design says it must NOT degrade. The design contradicts itself: it argues against typed-Unsupported-for-all because it would push users back to ifcopenshell, then specifies a manifold-ness gate that will push the same users to typed-Unsupported anyway. Either the gate is too strict (returning Unsupported on real input) or too lax (passing non-manifold to csgrs which then panics). There is no maintenance-cheap middle.

The design buries the actually-cheap maintenance option: ship typed Unsupported(reason) NOW with reveal-all + categorised stats, keep manifold behind the existing csg feature flag for desktop/CI, defer csgrs until the probe results are in. That is the lowest-debt path because it (a) reuses what we already have (manifold path, half-space pure-rust, reveal-all stance), (b) adds only the taxonomy (~100 LOC) without the kernel swap, (c) keeps the irreversible decision (drop manifold vs adopt csgrs) gated on real benchmark data. The proposed design commits to the kernel swap BEFORE the probe, which is the high-debt sequencing.
verdicts.suggested_mitigation:
Decouple the three orthogonal decisions the design conflates: (1) typed Unsupported taxonomy — ship NOW, ~100 LOC, low debt, no kernel choice required; (2) the four new tessellation handlers (tapered, boxed-halfspace, swept-disk, swept-area) — spin off as four independent GH issues, each shipped on its own merits, NOT bundled with the kernel question; (3) tier-B kernel choice — gate behind the csgrs-vs-manifold probe the design itself proposes (5–10 known-difficult files, measure crash rate, wrong-volume rate, perf). Until the probe lands, keep manifold as the sole tier-B engine behind the existing `csg` feature flag and treat GH#28 (linux-aarch64) as a separate workstream. If the probe shows csgrs >=95% match AND comparable perf, then commit fully to csgrs and DELETE manifold — do not run two engines in parallel forever. If the probe shows csgrs is not ready, keep manifold as primary and revisit in 6 months. The design's 'csgrs primary + manifold opt-in fallback-fallback' is the worst of both worlds maintenance-wise.</suggested_mitigation>
<parameter name="blocker">csgrs is a pre-1.0 hobby crate (231 stars, single primary maintainer, no published correctness benchmarks vs manifold) and the design commits to it as the primary tier-B kernel BEFORE running the production-readiness probe its own open-questions section says is required. If csgrs proves unreliable on Tekla/ArchiCAD input after we have already shipped the dispatch refactor, the UnsupportedReason taxonomy through PyO3/parquet/CLI, and the four new tessellation handlers, we own a permanent two-engine fork (csgrs + manifold-opt-in-fallback) that is strictly higher long-term maintenance debt than keeping manifold as the sole tier-B engine and fixing GH#28 directly. The maintenance argument the design makes ('lower-debt than manifold') is not supported by the bus-factor differential or by the 13 tuning-axis edge-cases the design itself enumerates.

missed_shapes: IfcFacetedBrep / IfcManifoldSolidBrep × IfcFacetedBrep cutter (brep × brep) — the audit lists Tekla compound voids and ArchiCAD complex openings as `occasional`, and general-fallback verdict implies it but no Phase 2 design treats brep-host × brep-cutter directly with a dedicated algorithm. It falls into 'general-fallback' but is geometrically distinct from 'arbitrary minus arbitrary' and has different validation requirements (both sides need manifold-validation, not just the cutter).

IfcMappedItem as host being cut (family/library instancing). Phase 1 calls this 'common' across all authoring tools. Mapped-item composite transform (t_target * t_origin) and inner-vs-outer caching semantics never appear in any design. Specifically: cross-product cut against a mapped representation's expanded mesh and how PrismParams / source tags survive the IfcRepresentationMap expansion.

IfcCsgSolid (root of IfcCsgPrimitive3D tree) as a HOST being cut. Phase 1 lists this with frequency `rare`-`occasional`. The composite csg_branch tag handling for HOST-side trees (not cutter-side) is unaddressed — only the cutter-side csg-primitive case got a design.

IfcFaceBasedSurfaceModel / IfcShellBasedSurfaceModel (fbsm/sbsm) as a host. Phase 1 says these are typically open-surface and 'not viable as cut hosts' — but Phase 2 has no explicit Outcome::UnsupportedOpenShell taxonomy or pre-flight detection at the dispatcher level (only mentioned in passing inside brep validator). Open-shell wrapped variants need a documented early-reject path.

IfcRevolvedAreaSolid as a HOST being cut (railings, curved walls, MEP fittings). Phase 1 marks `occasional`. Mentioned as a cutter edge case but never as a host — yet the audit says 'closed solid; viable as a cut host' so it's a real combination missing from the design matrix.

IfcGeometricCurveSet / IfcGeometricSet host. Phase 1 explicitly says 'an IfcWall whose body is a curveset is malformed' but no design handles the malformed case explicitly — should route to typed Unsupported(MalformedHost), not silent Fallback.

IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered as a HOST (tapered beam haunches in structural-steel). Phase 1 calls it `rare` and tracks it as unhandled. The general-fallback design adds a TAPERED CUTTER handler but never addresses what happens when a tapered extrusion is the HOST being cut by a regular extrusion or halfspace.

IfcSweptDiskSolid as a host being cut (MEP pipe with a tap/branch penetration). Phase 1 lists this as `common in MEP exports`. The general-fallback proposes a swept_disk.rs handler but never specifies what happens when a pipe IS THE HOST of a cut (e.g. a fitting subtracted from a pipe segment).

IfcAdvancedBrep as host (curved facades, NURBS-bounded). Phase 1 lists `rare` but real. The brep-minus-prism design handles AdvancedBrep as an edge case under the FacetedBrep umbrella but the design itself admits 'planar approximation tagged advanced_brep_approx — wrong for curved facades. As a cut host this can give wrong volumes silently.' No design produces a typed UnsupportedCurvedHost path.

IfcBoxedHalfSpace as a cutter. Phase 1 flags it as a SUBTYPE of IfcHalfSpaceSolid with explicit AABB, currently unhandled. Only the general-fallback addresses it tangentially; the bounded-halfspace category design does NOT include it — and it should slot directly there since semantically it IS a polygonal-bounded variant.

IfcBooleanResult with INTERSECTION operator producing actual intersection geometry (not reveal-all). No design proposes computing it; both UNION and INTERSECTION are degraded to passthrough or typed-Unsupported. The intersection case is legitimate IFC (wall = roof_volume ∩ wall_extrusion) and produces visibly wrong output as reveal-all.
IfcBooleanResult with UNION operator producing actual union geometry. Same gap — no design computes a real union, only passthrough.

Polygonal-bounded halfspace with TIGHT boundary (ifcopenshell-authored). The bounded-halfspace design explicitly admits this is the 'KNOWN GAP' but defers fixing it; no Phase 2 design closes it with a 2D-Boolean polygon-bounded variant as committed work.

Mixed IfcArbitraryProfileDefWithVoids host (cavity wall) × extrusion cutter — Phase 1 says profiles with voids exist, and the prism-prism design handles cutter-with-voids but doesn't show the host-with-voids case worked through (host outer + hole + cutter outer through both).

Concentric/coaxial multi-cutter case (two cylinders one inside the other, or telescoping openings) — listed as Phase 1 'concentric cutters' under csg-primitive but not consolidated as a general design pattern across categories.

Cutter is IfcCsgSolid wrapping IfcCsgPrimitive3D (Tekla MEP pattern). Phase 1 lists this; the csg-primitive design hand-waves dispatch via csg_branch tag chains but doesn't fully spec how nested csg_branch | csg_cylinder under CrossProductCut::flush is recognized.
IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid as cutter against any host. Phase 1 lists frequency `rare` but legal. The general-fallback proposes a host handler; no cutter-side treatment exists.
Multi-shell IfcFacetedBrep with disjoint shells inside one IfcClosedShell (ArchiCAD compound). The brep-minus-prism design defers to v2; no general design exists. It's a real combination.
missed_failure_modes: 'No validation of mesh winding before manifold call' (Phase 1 invariant violation) — only the brep-minus-prism design adds signed-volume validation. Prism-minus-prism, prism-minus-bounded-halfspace, and prism-minus-csg-primitive designs all assume outward-CCW winding without verifying. A CW-wound input collapses CSG semantics across the board.

'Empty result handling: subtract_many returns empty when cutters fully consume host. Call site must decide if that signals completion or error based on context' — no design specifies the empty-result Outcome variant convention. Each category handles it differently or hand-waves it. Cross-cutting policy decision missing.

'Pathological input: multiple edges sharing a start vertex degenerate case in boundary stitching, graph may fail to close, loops return incomplete, silent guard at line 334' — only bounded-halfspace verdict raises this; the cross-product-relvoids and general-fallback designs reuse halfspace_clip but don't account for the same vulnerability when stitching from THEIR new code paths.

'Coplanar face handling: no explicit coplanar-aware snap/merge in manifold — user must pre-merge or accept topological artifacts' — only brep-minus-prism partially addresses with a 1mm nudge (rejected by verdict). No design proposes a unified coplanar-snap pre-pass that all categories share.

'Deterministic vertex deduplication: manifold round-trips collapse coincident vertices and normalize topology consistently' — the pure-Rust replacements (i_overlay + csgrs + halfspace_clip composition) do NOT collectively guarantee this invariant. No design specifies a global vertex-weld policy at the kernel boundary.

'Non-manifold input rejection... Caller surfaces error rather than silently substituting fallback' — designs propose typed Unsupported variants per category but no unified taxonomy is committed across all five categories. The catch-as-net is inconsistent.

GH#28/1714 bbox-derived-tolerance behavior NOT in halfspaces — i.e. when manifold IS still used (general-fallback opt-in, csgrs fallback-fallback), the same tolerance-propagation pathology can re-emerge on brep cutters of unusual size; only flagged as 'worth a stress test' in brep design, never engineered against.

'Self-intersecting triangles' as a Phase 1 invariant violation — only general-fallback briefly mentions parry3d TriMesh self-intersection; no category commits to a pre-validation pass. Revit DOES emit these (composite-trees verdict named it as an unaddressed concern).

'IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered as cutter would prevent the cut' (Phase 1) — the general-fallback proposes a tapered HOST handler but the failure mode 'tapered cutter blocks ALL downstream cuts on the host' is never explicitly mitigated. Without a tapered-mesh handler at cutter position the entire downstream cut chain becomes Outcome::Fallback.
'IfcSurfaceCurveSweptAreaSolid as cutter blocks the cut' (Phase 1) — same gap.

'BaseSurface is not IfcPlane (cylindrical/spherical/BSpline)' — only the bounded-halfspace category acknowledges it as Optional hardening; no design commits typed UnsupportedNonPlanarBaseSurface across the pipeline.
'sub-mm sliver between cutter face and host face (Revit imprecise authoring)' — only mentioned in passing in prism-prism; no unified policy for sub-tolerance sliver handling across categories.

Cyclic IFC graph causing recursion stack overflow — composite-trees design lists this as 'Optional ~5 LOC' but the protection is never made mandatory and no other design picks it up. A single malformed file crashes the whole wheel.

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: unit_scale propagation through CSG tolerances. The substrate convention is source units (mm, m, ft). Multiple designs hard-code tolerances in mm (ON_PLANE_EPS=1e-3 mm, weld tolerance 1e-4 m, csgrs PLANE_EPSILON=1e-4 mm, i_overlay snap radius). NO design carries unit_scale into the kernel boundary. A US-imperial Revit project silently misbehaves across every category. This is a single shared fix (a UnitScaleAwareTolerances policy struct injected at dispatcher) that no design owns.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: ALL five categories (prism-minus-prism, bounded-halfspace, csg-primitive, brep-minus-prism, cross-product-relvoids, general-fallback)

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Source-tag preservation through the cut. Multiple designs build NEW geometry (e.g. prism-minus-prism re-extrudes from a 2D Boolean; bounded-halfspace earcut caps; csgrs polygon output). The source tags on the resulting mesh segments are inconsistently specified — some designs emit `cut_openings`, others overwrite, others append. The agent-facing contract (per Phase 1: source tag chain is how is_cutter / is_halfspace_cutter work) is broken across the pipeline. Need a unified post-cut tagging policy that preserves provenance for substrate/AGENTS.md.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: cross-cutting all five categories

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Far-from-origin precision when ParametricParams sidecars (PrismParams, ExtrusionParams) flow through the pipeline. mesh_anchor rebasing exists for vertex buffers but the prism-prism and cross-product-relvoids designs propose new sidecar metadata (profile origin, sweep direction, placement Mat4) that are NOT rebased — leaving them in raw f32 world coords for UTM-scale projects. The pipeline-wide invariant 'all coordinates in kernel inputs are in mesh_anchor-local frame' is violated by the parametric sidecars.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: prism-minus-prism, cross-product-relvoids, plus implicit interaction with composite-trees

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: BakeFrame::Local vs World — designs invoke world transforms (T = host.position.inverse() * world_xform_opening_to_host * cutter.position) without explicit mention of which frame BakeFrame is operating in. ifcfast's pipeline distinguishes Local (rep-origin-relative) vs World (project-relative); the cross-product-relvoids design conflates them. Without explicit policy, every category that touches PrismParams or recovers profile from clipped extrusion has undefined behavior under each BakeFrame setting.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: prism-minus-prism, cross-product-relvoids, composite-trees Tier C profile-recovery

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Instance-dedup (IfcRepresentationMap / mesh fingerprint) interaction. Cut output mesh is product-specific; if a representation is shared across instances and one instance has IfcRelVoidsElement and another does not, the cached representation MUST NOT be the post-cut mesh. NO design addresses cache invalidation semantics post-cut. Cross-product-relvoids in particular operates AFTER mesh assembly, meaning the substrate `representations.parquet` row is potentially wrong if the cut mutates it in place. The reveal-all stance helps but is silent on this.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all categories that perform a cut; specifically prism-minus-prism, cross-product-relvoids, csg-primitive cutter

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Fingerprint column accuracy post-cut. Per MEMORY.md fingerprint-columns, instances.parquet carries centroid_xyz / vertex_count / triangle_count from v0.4.19+. Replacing manifold with csgrs/i_overlay/parametric paths produces different mesh topology (potentially 10x triangle count per csgrs notes) for the same input. This is a determinism break in the substrate that downstream clash-control depends on. No design accounts for fingerprint-stability across kernel swaps.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all five categories

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Feature flag matrix explosion. Today there's `csg` (on/off). Designs propose `csg-pure`, `pure_csg`, `csg-manifold-fallback`, `prism-fast-path`, `prism-csg-fast`. Combined with the existing `mesh` and platform features, CI matrix grows combinatorially. No unified feature-flag plan exists across categories. Per MEMORY.md release-cross-compile, the existing 4-platform wheel matrix is already brittle.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all categories proposing pure-Rust paths

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: AGENTS.md / substrate schema drift. New Outcome variants (UnsupportedBrep, UnsupportedCutter, NonManifoldInput, CurvedSurfaceApproximated, DegenerateCutter, etc.), new sidecar fields (cut_strategy column on instances.parquet), and new stats counters all bump _CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION and must update AGENTS.md per project CLAUDE.md. No design takes responsibility for the unified taxonomy.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all five categories

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Parallel tessellation (rayon T=8 path) interaction with new kernels. csgrs is single-threaded and uses global allocator; i_overlay is single-threaded; brep_validate uses HashMap. T=8 worker contention on global heap is a known regression vector. No design owns a thread-local arena (bumpalo) policy across all the new transient allocations (welder HashMaps, polygon Vecs, BSP nodes).
cross_cutting_concerns.where: ALL categories that allocate transient buffers per cut

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Dispatcher recursion-depth cap is duplicated/missing. composite-trees design lists it as optional; no other category guarantees recursion safety. A single MAX_BOOLEAN_DEPTH constant should be enforced at the dispatcher entry, not deferred per-category.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: composite-trees + all category recursion points (CrossProductCut::flush, boolean::boolean_result, csg_solid)

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Cross-product (IfcRelVoidsElement) anchor synchronization. Cross-product flush translates cutter vertices by (opening.mesh_anchor - host.mesh_anchor) in f32. The cross-product-relvoids verdict identifies that host and cutter anchor selection is independent in the existing pipeline, so the delta may not be a clean ~mm offset. NO design proposes synchronized anchor selection for paired host/cutter prior to flush.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: cross-product-relvoids primarily, secondarily prism-prism and brep-prism when invoked via cross-product

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Test fixture corpus. Multiple designs explicitly list 'no real-world fixtures' as a gap (csg-primitive cutter test files = empty per audit, brep-prism test files = empty, multi-shell ArchiCAD never tested). No category commits to building a regression corpus BEFORE shipping; each design defers to 'benchmark before commit' separately. Need a centralized fixture-collection workstream.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: ALL categories

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Outcome::Fallback (reveal-all) cumulative semantics across categories. Today's Fallback at one stage can interact with a downstream stage that ALSO fallbacks. The composition (halfspace_clip succeeds → csgrs fallbacks → reveal-all of post-halfspace mesh) is not the same as the original reveal-all-of-input. No design specifies the canonical reveal-all-state when stages compose.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: composite-trees, cross-product-relvoids, general-fallback

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: PyO3 / parquet / CLI surface for the new typed Unsupported variants. Multiple designs propose HashMap<Enum, usize> stats. The cross-FFI serialization story is acknowledged as 'open question' but never committed. Adding stats fields is a schema change that needs cache_schema_version bump in lockstep with AGENTS.md.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all categories proposing Unsupported variants

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Library bus-factor compounding. Designs adopt i_overlay (single-maintainer, pre-1.0), csgrs (231 stars, pre-1.0, CSG.js port), and keep manifold (one fragile maintainer, C++ build pain) as opt-in. NET bus-factor across the pipeline = max of 3 fragile deps instead of 1. No design owns a unified vendor-or-fork policy.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: prism-prism (i_overlay), brep-prism (csgrs), cross-product (i_overlay + csgrs), general-fallback (csgrs + manifold)

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Tag-chain encoding insufficiency. composite-trees verdict identifies that mesh::boolean::retag uses `role.unwrap_or(new_role)` semantics (innermost-wins), preserving only TWO tokens, NOT the multi-link chain the design assumes. Every design that reads is_cutter/is_halfspace_cutter assumes a chain. This is a foundational bug that invalidates partition logic across multiple categories.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: composite-trees, bounded-halfspace, csg-primitive, cross-product-relvoids — every category that scans source chain tags

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Polygon fill-rule choice (EvenOdd vs NonZero) for i_overlay across all 2D-Boolean call sites. prism-prism picks NonZero, cross-product-relvoids picks EvenOdd, bounded-halfspace picks NonZero (implied). The choice changes correctness on IfcArbitraryProfileDefWithVoids host. Different categories pick different fill rules — silent topology divergence across the pipeline.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: prism-minus-prism, prism-minus-bounded-halfspace (if polygon-bounded variant ships), cross-product-relvoids, csg-primitive (if Case-B ships)

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: csgrs version pinning + nalgebra version unification. csgrs depends on nalgebra+parry3d at versions that may diverge from ifcfast's existing parry3d (already used in geom feature for clash). Version conflict forces duplicate compilation or pins existing crate back. No design owns the dependency-graph resolution.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: brep-prism, general-fallback, anywhere csgrs lands

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Mesh segment range alignment invariant. composite-trees verdict notes assemble_submesh indexes seg.index_start..index_count without asserting `index_start % 3 == 0` and `index_count % 3 == 0`. A malformed segment range shifts triangle winding and inverts derived plane normals. This is a shared invariant across every design that uses MeshSegment ranges and is never centrally enforced.
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all categories using MeshSegment partition

cross_cutting_concerns.concern: Outcome ordering when multiple cuts compose on one host (halfspace → prism-cutter → csg-primitive → brep-cutter). The dispatcher order is implicit in current cut_openings.rs:115-140 but each design proposes route adjustments. No design specifies a canonical composition order or invariant about what intermediate mesh state must hold between stages (closed-manifold? oriented? etc.).
cross_cutting_concerns.where: all five categories — dispatcher is shared

recommended_additions: Add an explicit 'IfcMappedItem as host' design — how mapped representations participate in cuts; how PrismParams / source tags survive IfcRepresentationMap expansion; how the cached inner-representation invalidation works post-cut.

Add a 'BakeFrame contract' specification — for every parametric metadata sidecar (PrismParams, ExtrusionParams, ClippedExtrusionParams), define the coordinate frame (Local/World/MeshAnchor) it lives in and how the kernel boundary converts. Make this a debug-assert at every kernel adapter.

Add a unified UnitScaleAwareTolerances policy struct — a single source of truth for ON_PLANE_EPS, weld tolerance, axis-alignment epsilon, snap-grid resolution, etc., parameterized by source unit_scale. Inject at dispatcher entry, propagate to every leaf primitive.

Add a unified Outcome / UnsupportedReason taxonomy — single enum covering NonManifoldInput / SelfIntersectingCutter / CoplanarFaceDegeneracy / DegenerateCutter / CurvedSurfaceApproximated / KernelInternalError / IntersectionNotImplemented / UnionNotImplemented / NonPlanarBaseSurface / UnhandledCutterEntity / MalformedHost / BspDepthExceeded / TightPolygonalBoundary. Owned ONCE, threaded through stats + parquet + PyO3 + CLI + AGENTS.md.

Refactor mesh::boolean::retag from innermost-wins to true chain accumulation (SmallVec<[&'static str; 4]> or Vec<String>) BEFORE any category that depends on chain semantics ships. This is foundational and currently fictional.

Add a unified pre-cut validation gate at the dispatcher — manifold-ness (closed-edge), winding consistency (signed-volume sign), self-intersection (parry3d TriMesh), degenerate triangle filter (area > unit-scaled epsilon), self-loops in IFC graph (recursion-depth cap). Single module geom::cut_validate.rs.

Add a unified post-cut invariant check — volume conservation oracle: result_signed_volume ∈ [host_signed_volume − Σ cutter_signed_volume_∩_host, host_signed_volume]. On violation, fall to next-tier kernel or typed Unsupported. Mandatory across all categories.
Add a unified anchor synchronization policy for cross-product cuts — force host and cutter to share mesh_anchor before flush. Add to mesh::cut_openings::flush as a precondition.

Add a unified vertex-weld pass between stages — at every stage transition (halfspace_clip → prism_cutter → csg_primitive → general_csg), run a unit-scaled spatial-hash weld to ensure inter-stage manifoldness. Single module geom::weld.rs.

Add a unified thread-local arena policy — bumpalo per rayon worker for transient polygon Vecs, BSP nodes, HashMap reuse across cuts within one product. Required for T=8 parallel scaling to survive kernel replacement.
Add an explicit MAX_BOOLEAN_DEPTH cap (e.g., 64) enforced at the dispatcher entry — currently optional per composite-trees, needs to be mandatory and shared.

Add a 'feature flag plan' document — single workspace feature `csg-pure` vs `csg` (manifold), with explicit retirement plan for manifold and a CI matrix dimension count budget. Currently each design defines its own flag.

Add a 'fixture corpus' workstream — centralized collection of (a) Tekla precast brep, (b) ArchiCAD complex roof with halfspace stack, (c) Revit recess flush back-face, (d) ifcopenshell tight-bounded halfspace, (e) MEP swept-disk, (f) Sannergata-class deep halfspace + cross-product, (g) UTM-scale far-origin, (h) imperial unit-scale, (i) multi-shell ArchiCAD, (j) figure-8 self-intersecting cutter, (k) zero-area degenerate cutter, (l) cyclic IFC graph. Required gate before any kernel swap commits.

Add a 'fingerprint stability' contract — substrate consumers depend on deterministic vertex_count/triangle_count post-cut. Either (a) commit to a single canonical kernel and lock its output, or (b) version the fingerprint encoding so consumers can detect kernel change. Otherwise cache invalidation across kernel swaps silently breaks.

Adopt the prior-art pattern from ifcopenshell-CGAL's halfspace_tree decomposition (currently dormant) as a model for handling deep halfspace stacks. ifcfast is closer to the principled outcome than CGAL is — codify the pattern so future regressions don't re-invent.

Adopt the ifcopenshell-OCCT iterative-flattening pattern for deep halfspace chains (8+ operands). Phase 1 tools_surveyed comparison shows this is the principled prior art; the composite-trees flattening trick is the right move but the design should explicitly cite the rate-limiting pattern.

Adopt web-ifc's cutter-merging pattern for stacked halfspaces — pre-merge multiple bounded halfspace cutters into a single 2D-polygon-union before clipping, amortising per-cut cost. Phase 1 tools_surveyed shows this is web-ifc's actual production approach.

Investigate i_overlay's polygon-with-holes API contract once (open question in prism-prism is unresolved) — produces a single shared crate-internal helper used by prism-prism, cross-product-relvoids, csg-primitive Case-B, and bounded-halfspace polygon-bounded variant.

Decide INTERSECTION operator handling once: either compute via csgrs::intersect or surface Unsupported(IntersectionNotImplemented). DO NOT silently reveal-all (current composite-trees + general-fallback proposal is wrong). Add to the dispatcher contract.

Decide UNION operator handling once: either compute via real union_many primitive (and ship it) or surface Unsupported(UnionNotImplemented). Reveal-all is acceptable only when the design proves the operands don't overlap. Otherwise it produces interpenetrating shells.

Add 'cut_strategy: Utf8' provenance column to instances.parquet — surfaced from each kernel path so corpus analysis can confirm classifier hit rates and detect silent regressions. Cross-product-relvoids open-questioned this; promote to required.

Specify the fill-rule canonical choice (EvenOdd vs NonZero) across all 2D-Boolean call sites with a doc-test that demonstrates correct hole semantics for IfcArbitraryProfileDefWithVoids host. Currently each category picks independently.

Add a 'silent guard' audit — every design has 'silent passthrough on degenerate input' as a known behavior (stitch_boundary_loops, derive_plane_from_slab returning None, empty fragment lists, missing geometry). Promote every silent guard to a typed stats counter so consumers can detect them.

Address the latent `wall - (block - cylinder)` over-subtraction bug — composite-trees acknowledges it but leaves heuristic detection; needs proper sub-tree marker (boolean_subtree_open/close) in the chain encoding to soundly partition.

Define the empty-result Outcome contract: when subtract returns empty (cutter contains host), is it Outcome::Cut(empty) or Outcome::Unsupported(HostConsumed)? Currently each category picks differently.

Plan a 'csgrs production-readiness probe' as a hard gate before any pure-Rust general 3D CSG kernel adoption. 5-10 representative files (Sannergata, Duplex, Tekla precast, ArchiCAD complex roof). Measure crash rate, wrong-volume rate, perf vs manifold. If <95% match or >2x slowdown, defer csgrs and keep manifold behind feature flag.

Add a benchmark-gate policy: every new pure-Rust path must publish wall-clock per cut + total bundle time + peak RSS + allocation count via dhat on a canonical fixture before being promoted to default. No design currently commits to this gate explicitly.

Schedule manifold retirement explicitly. Either commit to a sunset date for the `csg` feature, OR codify keeping manifold indefinitely as the brep-prism + general-fallback engine while pure-Rust handles dominant cases. The 'mixed' recommendations across multiple categories leave this undecided.

Add an 'IfcOpenShell tessellation profile' compatibility note — IFC4 Reference View files (newer Revit defaults) emit IfcPolygonalFaceSet / IfcTriangulatedFaceSet for hosts. Confirm csgrs handles flat-shaded face-set hosts identically to faceted breps, since the audit lumps them but the topology differs.

Add a dedicated 'fallback chain' specification — when Tier A fails, does it go to Tier B (csgrs) or directly to typed Unsupported? When csgrs fails, does it fall back to manifold (if feature on) or directly to reveal-all? Each design proposes a different ladder; unify.

plan.executive_summary:
**Decision: do not replace manifold wholesale in this cycle. Keep manifold behind the `csg` feature as the production engine for the irreducible 3D-CSG cases (brep×brep, general fallback) and progressively retire it from the cases that admit a principled pure-Rust algorithm — starting with the half-space path that is already pure-Rust, then prism−prism via a 2D-Boolean+extrude path, then prism−bounded-halfspace via a polygon-bounded variant. The audit and verdicts converge on this: the prior-art consensus (ifcopenshell-OCCT, ifcopenshell-CGAL, web-ifc, Bonsai) is that 3D mesh CSG is the cost everyone pays for the general case; only halfspace and prism-aligned cases reduce to cheaper algebraic primitives. The right move is to ship those reductions cleanly and leave manifold as the long-tail engine, not to chase csgrs as a full manifold replacement on the back of a pre-1.0 BSP port with documented coplanar-face fragility.**
