The Phase-3 verdicts refute the most ambitious designs across all four lenses. Prism−prism via slab-decomposition + vertex weld was refuted on correctness (non-manifold seams) and robustness (UTM-scale f32 precision unaccounted for in PrismParams). Bounded-halfspace was refuted because the *polygonal* boundary is currently ignored — the "97.4% Sannergata match" is statistical luck that the Revit oversize convention happens to align with infinite-plane cut semantics. csg-primitive Case-B was refuted on the worked example (silhouette+re-extrude is geometrically wrong for perpendicular MEP cylinders, which IS the dominant case). Brep-prism's csgrs proposal was refuted on every axis (Tekla T-junctions, coplanar pathology, missing self-intersection check, BSP O(N²) worst case). Cross-product-relvoids' Tier A was refuted because the classifier rejects the canonical perpendicular-cutter authoring pattern. General-fallback's INTERSECTION-as-reveal-all is silently wrong by ~2×-volume.

The cross-cutting critic surfaced the more dangerous problem: the **chain-tag mental model is fiction**. `mesh::boolean::retag` is `role.unwrap_or(new_role)` — innermost-wins, only two tokens preserved. Every design that scans the source chain for `boolean_second_operand` × N depth, or for sub-tree provenance, is reading a chain that does not exist in the codebase. This is foundational. Until `retag` is refactored to accumulate a real chain (SmallVec<&'static str>), composite-trees correctness claims are untestable. Likewise, unit_scale is not propagated to any kernel tolerance — US-imperial Revit files silently misbehave. And `PrismParams`/`ExtrusionParams` sidecars in the rejected designs were specified without a BakeFrame / mesh_anchor contract, guaranteeing far-origin corruption on every Norwegian UTM project.

The right plan is therefore inversion: invest in **shared infrastructure first** (real chain encoding, unit-aware tolerances, validation gate, unified Outcome taxonomy, fixture corpus), then ship *narrow, well-validated* pure-Rust paths (typed Unsupported for UNION/INTERSECTION/unhandled cutters; polygon-bounded halfspace with i_overlay; tapered-extrusion handler; tight prism−prism with explicit precondition gates), and only consider csgrs after a hard production-readiness probe on the corpus. The `csg` feature stays default-on indefinitely as the safety net; "pure-Rust only" becomes opt-in via `pure-rust-csg` feature whose coverage grows release by release. Manifold retires from individual categories as their pure-Rust paths are proved on real fixtures, not on the promise of a single big-bang swap.

**What would change this decision**: (1) If the tester confirms GH #56 is NOT manifold's fault (it's a winding/non-manifold-brep upstream issue), the case for keeping manifold strengthens further and the priority should shift fully to upstream validation gates rather than kernel replacement. (2) If a probe shows csgrs achieves ≥95% match against manifold on the Sannergata/Duplex/Tekla corpus AND ≤2× slowdown, the calculus flips toward csgrs-as-default — but until that probe exists, this is speculation. (3) If linux-aarch64 wheel-build (GH #28) becomes a hard customer requirement, a narrow "pure-Rust-only" wheel becomes mandatory and the priority order moves W7/W8 up. (4) If `IfcOpenShell` 0.9+ ships a stable Rust-bindings IFC kernel, the whole question reframes as "wrap or write".

sequenced_work_items.title: Real source-chain encoding (foundational refactor)
sequenced_work_items.scope: Refactor `mesh::boolean::retag` (crates/core/src/mesh/boolean.rs:297-308) and `MeshFragment.source` from innermost-wins `Option<&'static str>` + `format!('{}|{}', role, source)` two-token construction into true chain accumulation. Replace with `SmallVec<[&'static str; 6]>` or equivalent. Update all readers: `cut_openings::is_cutter`, `is_halfspace_cutter`, `is_prism_cutter` (planned), and any source.split('|') call site (audit shows 4-5 sites in cut_openings.rs and stats.rs). Add a unit-tested helper `chain_contains(seg, link) -> bool` and `chain_count(seg, link) -> usize`. Touched: crates/core/src/mesh/boolean.rs, crates/core/src/mesh/mod.rs (the format! at ~line 817), crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (chain readers), crates/core/src/mesh/stats.rs.
sequenced_work_items.rollout: default-on; this is a behavior-preserving refactor verified by existing integration tests (deep_bcr_with_three_halfspaces, cut_openings_produces_single_segment, cross_product_voids_fold). Snapshot tests on chain token sequences must be added BEFORE the refactor.

sequenced_work_items.title: Unified Outcome / UnsupportedReason taxonomy + stats
sequenced_work_items.scope: Promote `cut_openings::Outcome` from {Cut, Passthrough, Fallback} to {Cut, Passthrough, Fallback, Unsupported(UnsupportedReason)}. Define `UnsupportedReason` enum with variants: NonManifoldInput, SelfIntersectingCutter, CoplanarFaceDegeneracy, KernelInternalError, CurvedSurfaceApproximated, IntersectionNotImplemented, UnionWithOverlap, NonPlanarBaseSurface, UnhandledCutterEntity(&'static str), MalformedHost, BspDepthExceeded, TightPolygonalBoundaryIgnored, DegenerateCutter, HostConsumed. Add per-reason counters to `CutOpeningsStats`. Surface through lib.rs accumulators (3 existing call sites). PyO3 surface: flat counter fields, NOT HashMap (FFI-friendly). Bump `_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION`. Update AGENTS.md taxonomy section. Touched: crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (Outcome enum + dispatch sites), crates/core/src/mesh/stats.rs, crates/core/src/lib.rs (accumulators), python/ifcfast/header.py (_CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION), AGENTS.md, README.md.
sequenced_work_items.rollout: default-on; backward-compatible (Fallback remains catch-all for unrecognised failure paths). PyO3 surface gains fields but doesn't break existing readers.

sequenced_work_items.title: Unit-aware tolerance policy + cross-cutting validation gate
sequenced_work_items.scope: Create `crates/core/src/geom/tolerance.rs` with a single `CsgTolerances { unit_scale: f32, on_plane_eps: f32, weld_eps: f32, axis_eps: f32, area_eps: f32, snap_grid: f32 }` policy struct. Replace hard-coded `ON_PLANE_EPS = 1e-3` in halfspace_clip.rs and any other site with policy lookups parameterised by source unit_scale. Add `crates/core/src/geom/cut_validate.rs`: closed-edge check (sign-sum on directed-edge HashMap), signed-volume sign check (divergence theorem), degenerate-triangle filter (area > tolerance.area_eps), self-loop detector (parry3d TriMesh-self-intersection sample), MAX_BOOLEAN_DEPTH=64 dispatcher cap. Wire validator at every kernel adapter entry (halfspace_clip, csg::subtract_many, future prism_csg). On failure → Outcome::Unsupported(reason). Touched: NEW crates/core/src/geom/tolerance.rs, NEW crates/core/src/geom/cut_validate.rs, crates/core/src/mesh/halfspace_clip.rs (tolerance plumbing), crates/core/src/geom/csg.rs (pre-validate), crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (dispatcher cap + tolerance plumbing).
sequenced_work_items.rollout: default-on; behavior change is that today's silent Fallback cases become typed Unsupported(reason). Validate against existing test corpus (Sannergata, Duplex) — fallback rate should not REGRESS. Tester sweep gate.

sequenced_work_items.title: Operator-aware IfcBooleanResult (UNION/INTERSECTION semantics)
sequenced_work_items.scope: Currently `mesh::boolean::boolean_result` ignores fields[0]. Parse the operator: `.DIFFERENCE.` → current behavior, `.UNION.` → emit `Outcome::Unsupported(UnionWithOverlap)` UNLESS we can prove non-overlap via AABB disjoint check (then reveal-all both operands tagged `union_lhs`/`union_rhs`), `.INTERSECTION.` → `Outcome::Unsupported(IntersectionNotImplemented)`. Add a typed `boolean_op:{difference|union|intersect}` link to the chain (W1 dependency) for downstream provenance. Update is_cutter/is_halfspace_cutter to ignore union/intersect roles. Touched: crates/core/src/mesh/boolean.rs (boolean_result + retag callsites), crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (chain readers), tests for synthetic IfcBooleanResult UNION + INTERSECTION fixtures.

Each fixture is a small synthetic IFC + an expected-outcome assertion (volume, mesh_quality, Outcome variant). Add integration test runner in crates/core/tests/cut_openings_corpus.rs. sequenced_work_items.rollout: CI gate; required before any kernel-swap PR is merged.

sequenced_work_items.title: Polygon-bounded halfspace correctness fix (the KNOWN GAP)
sequenced_work_items.scope: Close the bounded-halfspace audit gap: when the polygonal boundary is sized tighter than the host AABB, the current `clip_by_plane` over-cuts. Two-phase: (1) Add a polygon-tightness discriminator at parse time in `mesh::boolean::polygonal_bounded_halfspace`: compute polygon AABB diagonal vs host AABB diagonal. If polygon < 0.7 × host AABB diagonal, route to the new tight path. (2) Implement the tight path via `i_overlay` v7 (pure Rust, Apache-2/MIT): slice the host on the BaseSurface plane via halfspace_clip's existing primitives to recover the cut polygon, project it and the IfcPolygonalBoundary onto BaseSurface.Position.XY, compute the 2D intersection via `Overlay::with_subj_and_clip(...).overlay(OverlayRule::Difference, FillRule::NonZero)`, only remove host volume where intersection is non-empty. Otherwise (polygon ≥ 0.7 × host diagonal, i.e. Revit oversize convention) keep current infinite-plane behavior. Harden stitch_boundary_loops to true edge-disjoint walk (HashMap<u32, Vec<u32>>). Add i_overlay as a workspace dep. Touched: crates/core/Cargo.toml, crates/core/src/mesh/boolean.rs (polygonal_bounded_halfspace), crates/core/src/mesh/halfspace_clip.rs (stitch_boundary_loops), NEW crates/core/src/mesh/polygon_bool.rs (i_overlay wrapper).
sequenced_work_items.rollout: default-on; closes a known correctness gap. Validation by fixture (g).

sequenced_work_items.title: Anchor synchronization for cross-product cuts
sequenced_work_items.scope: Currently `CrossProductCut::flush` translates cutter vertices by `(opening.mesh_anchor - host.mesh_anchor) as f32`. Anchors are chosen independently per product, so the delta can be ~10m due to anchor-snap discretization, not a clean ~mm offset, and the f32 truncation at UTM scale loses 5-6 significant digits. Fix: at the moment the IfcRelVoidsElement relationship is resolved, force the cutter's mesh_anchor to equal the host's mesh_anchor by re-rebasing cutter vertices to the host anchor (in f64 then truncate). Add a debug-assert that paired host+cutter mesh_anchor are equal at the entry to flush. Touched: crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (CrossProductCut::flush + the mesh-anchor selection point upstream), crates/core/src/mesh/mod.rs (anchor selection for opening products).
sequenced_work_items.rollout: default-on; bug fix. Validate on fixture (j) — UTM-rebased far-origin wall.

sequenced_work_items.title: Prism-prism 2D-Boolean fast path (narrow, gated)
sequenced_work_items.scope: Implement the prism-minus-prism fast path RESTRICTED to the through-cut case (Case A.6 'through-cut' from the Phase 2 design): host extrusion + cutter extrusion + axes parallel within axis_eps + cutter sweep interval covers host sweep interval. NO slab decomposition (the verdict rightly refuted the slab+weld approach as non-manifold). For partial-recess and pocket cases: fall through to manifold via the existing csg::subtract_many. Algorithm: project cutter profile to host's profile plane (rebased to mesh_anchor in f64, narrow to f32 at i_overlay boundary), compute 2D difference via i_overlay (NonZero fill — documented), re-extrude via refactored `triangulate_extrusion(outer, holes, dir, depth, position) -> LocalMesh`. PrismParams sidecar threaded ONLY through the through-cut path. Pre-flight gate: signed-volume sign check on host, axis snap-to-parallel within 1e-3 rad (else fall to manifold), unit-aware tolerances throughout. Behind `prism-csg-fast` feature flag, default OFF. Touched: crates/core/Cargo.toml, NEW crates/core/src/mesh/prism_csg.rs, crates/core/src/mesh/extrusion.rs (extract triangulate_extrusion helper + PrismParams export), crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs (dispatcher), tests including fixtures (a)(b)(j)(m).
sequenced_work_items.rollout: feature-flag opt-in. Gate promotion to default on benchmark: ≥2× faster than manifold on fixtures (a)(b) AND no correctness regression on full corpus AND fallback rate to manifold ≤30% (i.e., the fast path actually catches ≥70% of door/window cuts). If gate fails, retire the feature flag rather than ship slow.

For each IfcRelVoidsElement / IfcBooleanClippingResult pair: run manifold and csgrs side-by-side, record (a) crash/panic rate, (b) volume agreement (output_volume_manifold vs output_volume_csgrs within 0.1%), (c) topology stats (triangle count, vertex count, mesh_quality classifier output), (d) wall-clock per cut, (e) peak RSS. Publish results as a worklog. Acceptance gate for csgrs as a tier-B kernel: ≥95% volume agreement, 0 panics, ≤3× median wall-clock. DOES NOT ADOPT csgrs yet — produces the data we need to decide. plan.feature_flag_strategy:
**Current state**: `default = [..., "csg"]` where `csg` brings in manifold-csg (C++ dep).

**Target state through the plan**:

- **Releases shipping W1–W8** (foundation + correctness fixes): `default = [..., "csg"]` UNCHANGED. Manifold remains the production engine. All improvements (real chain encoding, unit-aware tolerances, validation gate, operator-aware IfcBooleanResult, polygon-bounded halfspace, anchor sync, tapered handler, typed Unsupported taxonomy) are default-on. No new feature flag introduced yet. This is critical: the whole foundation phase must be feature-flag-free so users get the correctness fixes immediately and CI matrix stays at today's count.

- **Releases shipping W9+W12** (prism-prism + csg-primitive fast paths): introduce `prism-csg-fast` Cargo feature, default OFF. Mutually compatible with `csg`. Users opt in for the speedup; manifold remains the safety net. The feature is promoted to default-on only after a benchmark gate (≥2× speedup on dominant fixtures, ≤30% fallback-to-manifold rate, zero correctness regressions). If the gate fails on the first attempt, the feature stays opt-in indefinitely or is retired — we do NOT ship slow.

- **Release shipping W15** (post-csgrs-probe decision): conditional on W10 outcome. If csgrs passes the probe: introduce `pure-rust-csg` feature, default OFF, MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with `csg` (Cargo's `[features]` cfg). This gives linux-aarch64 and pure-rust-only users a path; everyone else stays on manifold. The default does NOT change. If csgrs fails: no new feature flag; `csg` stays sole tier-B engine.

- **Long-term target**: `default = [..., "csg", "prism-csg-fast"]`. `csg` (manifold) remains optional but on-by-default for at minimum 2 minor releases past the prism-csg-fast promotion. `pure-rust-csg` stays opt-in for users who explicitly cannot ship C++ deps. **Manifold is NOT retired** in this cycle — its removal is a separate decision gated on a future audit showing pure-Rust coverage is empirically complete on real corpora.

**Anti-goals**: We do NOT ship a dual-engine path where csgrs and manifold are both active in the same default build (the brep-prism verdict and maintenance-cost critic both refute this as double-debt). Either manifold OR csgrs serves tier-B at runtime, decided by Cargo features at build time. The CI matrix expands by at most 1 dimension across this whole plan.

**CI matrix**: today is 4 platforms × 1 feature config (default). Plan adds: `+ prism-csg-fast` config once W9 lands (4 × 2 = 8). After W15 if csgrs is adopted: `+ pure-rust-csg` config (4 × 3 = 12). MEMORY.md release-cross-compile flags wheel matrix fragility — adding 8 build jobs is the budget; we do NOT exceed it. If a feature would push past 12 we retire one (e.g. retire `prism-csg-fast` flag once promoted to default).
open_questions: Does GH #56 (the open tester report) implicate manifold's CSG kernel, or is it an upstream non-manifold-brep / winding bug that would be caught by W11's pre-flight validator? Answer reshapes priority between W11 (high) and W15 (deferred or accelerated).

i_overlay v7 polygon-with-holes API: confirm Overlay::with_subj_and_clip accepts polygons-with-holes as a single shape with NonZero fill, OR requires EvenOdd, OR requires separate add_shape calls. Blocks W6 and W9 implementation detail. Answerable in 1 hour of doc-reading + a synthetic test.

i_overlay f32 vs scaled-i64 robustness: is the f32 API safe at building-scale (10 m walls, 1 mm tolerance) after mesh_anchor rebase, or do we need to scale by 1000 and use i32/i64? Affects W6 and W9. Resolvable via fixture (g) bounded-tightly + close-spaced openings stress.
csgrs production-readiness on real IFC input (W10's whole point). Pre-condition for the W15 decision. Resolvable only by running the probe.

Should the prism-prism fast path (W9) cover the partial-recess and pocket cases via per-instance seam reconstruction (rejected by the verdict in the slab-decomposition form), or stay narrowly through-cut-only and route partial cases to manifold? Decision: narrow now, evaluate seam reconstruction after corpus measures the partial-recess frequency.

BakeFrame Local vs World: does cross-product-relvoids' anchor synchronization (W7) interact with World-frame substrate output? The cut output is mesh_anchor-local; the substrate writer composes mesh_anchor + product placement to world. Verify no regression in fixture (j).

Should we adopt the ifcopenshell-OCCT iterative-flattening pattern for deep halfspace chains (8+ operands) as a defensive measure inside halfspace_clip's sequential loop? Current sequential approach works for Sannergata; the question is whether it survives an even-deeper stack. Defer until evidence of failure.

For IfcExtrudedAreaSolidTapered as a CUTTER: ship a handler in W8 or only typed-Unsupported it? Real-world frequency essentially zero; default to typed-Unsupported, ship handler only on customer demand.

For multi-shell IfcFacetedBrep (ArchiCAD): does W11's per-connected-component winding fix actually let manifold accept the input, or does manifold require single-shell input? Answer determines whether we ship a connected-components subtract-and-merge wrapper or surface MultiShellNotSupported.

recommended_first_pr.title: feat(csg): unified Outcome::Unsupported taxonomy + real source-chain encoding (W1+W2 foundation)
recommended_first_pr.scope: Single PR combining the two foundational refactors that everything else depends on. Files touched: (1) crates/core/src/mesh/boolean.rs — refactor MeshFragment.source from `Option<&'static str>` + format! two-token construction into SmallVec<[&'static str; 6]> chain accumulation in retag(). Add `chain_contains(seg, link)` and `chain_count(seg, link)` helpers. (2) crates/core/src/mesh/mod.rs — update the `format!("{}|{}", role, source)` site (~line 817) to use chain accumulation. (3) crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs — update is_cutter, is_halfspace_cutter, and all source.split('|') readers to use the new helpers. Add the Outcome::Unsupported(UnsupportedReason) variant; UnsupportedReason enum with initial variants {NonManifoldInput, SelfIntersectingCutter, CoplanarFaceDegeneracy, KernelInternalError, CurvedSurfaceApproximated, IntersectionNotImplemented, UnionWithOverlap, NonPlanarBaseSurface, UnhandledCutterEntity(&'static str), MalformedHost, BspDepthExceeded, TightPolygonalBoundaryIgnored, DegenerateCutter, HostConsumed}. (4) crates/core/src/mesh/stats.rs — extend CutOpeningsStats with per-reason counter fields (flat, not HashMap, for FFI). (5) crates/core/src/lib.rs — update the 3 stats-accumulator call sites. (6) python/ifcfast/header.py — bump _CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION. (7) AGENTS.md — document the new taxonomy + chain encoding. (8) Tests: add chain snapshot tests in crates/core/src/mesh/cut_openings.rs::tests asserting the chain token sequences on the existing deep_bcr_with_three_halfspaces, cut_openings_produces_single_segment, and cross_product_voids_fold fixtures. Add one new unit test exercising chain_count for a 3-deep synthetic boolean.
recommended_first_pr.why_first: Three reasons. First, the chain-encoding refactor is foundational — the completeness critic surfaced that the existing chain mental model is fiction (innermost-wins, two tokens only). Every subsequent design that reads is_cutter at a non-trivial depth (composite-trees, cross-product-relvoids, csg-primitive dispatch, future prism_cutter dispatch) is built on a chain encoding that does not exist. Shipping anything else first would mean coding against the fiction and discovering it later. Second, the Outcome::Unsupported taxonomy is the single highest-leverage agent-facing improvement: today's opaque silent Fallback becomes a typed signal the agent layer can route on, with zero behavior change for callers that don't inspect the reason. This is the kind of thing AGENTS.md upkeep is for and a clean win on the user-feedback surface even if nothing else ever ships. Third, this PR is small (~400 LOC plus tests + docs), behavior-preserving for the happy path (the existing test suite validates the refactor), and the schema bump is justified by net new agent-readable surface — no consumer breakage. It's exactly the small, low-risk, foundation-laying PR the plan demands at the front. Everything from W3 onward links cleanly off this base.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Chain-encoding refactor (W1) breaks existing tag-based dispatch in subtle ways — is_cutter / is_halfspace_cutter / is_prism_cutter (future) read the chain at different depths. Snapshot tests of token sequences before refactor + integration test coverage on Sannergata-class fixtures is the only mitigation.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Land snapshot tests of the existing chain output (innermost-wins two-token strings) BEFORE the refactor PR. Tag the snapshot test as documenting the OLD behavior, then update it to the new full-chain form in the same PR. Run the full integration test suite (deep_bcr_with_three_halfspaces, cut_openings_produces_single_segment, cross_product_voids_fold, complex_nested_boolean_tree) in CI before merge. If any production fixture regresses, revert and ship as an additive-only Vec<String> sidecar (more LOC, lower risk).

risks_and_mitigations.risk: i_overlay v7 API instability (single-maintainer, pre-1.0 churn from 6.x to 7.x). Adopting it across W6, W9, W12 creates a soft dep on a moving target. A breaking change in 7.x→8.x lands in the middle of our migration.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Pin to a specific 7.x version in Cargo.toml. Wrap i_overlay behind a thin internal facade (`crates/core/src/mesh/polygon_bool.rs`) so swapping it for geo-clipper or a fork is a one-file change. Re-evaluate the dep at each major release. Consider vendoring if the upstream goes stale (~2.5k LOC).

risks_and_mitigations.risk: csgrs production-readiness probe (W10) reveals csgrs cannot handle real corpus — coplanar faces fail, BSP depth exceeded on deep trees, panics on Tekla T-junctions. The whole pure-Rust-replacement track has no engine at the long-tail end.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: This is exactly why W10 is a probe, not adoption. The decision in W15 is binary: if csgrs fails, retire the adoption track for this cycle and keep manifold indefinitely. The foundation work (W1-W8, W11) is independently valuable — typed Unsupported, polygon-bounded halfspace fix, validator gate, anchor sync, fixture corpus — and ships regardless. Manifold's CMake/Windows wheel pain is a known, bounded problem to triage separately.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: W9 prism-prism fast path benchmark gate fails: in real corpus it catches <70% of cuts or is <2× faster than manifold. The feature ships as opt-in dead code.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Define the gate explicitly BEFORE landing W9 (≥2× speedup on door+window fixtures, ≤30% fallback rate, zero correctness regression). If gate fails, retire the prism-csg-fast feature flag rather than promote it. Document the negative result in the worklog so future contributors don't re-attempt without new evidence. Manifold remains the default for prism-prism.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Unit-aware tolerance policy (W3) introduces subtle behavior change in halfspace_clip's ON_PLANE_EPS — files in feet/inches start producing different cut geometry than the same files did under the hard-coded 1e-3 mm constant. US-imperial users see visible change.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Frame the change as a bug fix (today's hard-coded mm is wrong for non-metric files), document in the worklog and AGENTS.md, ship with a debug log of the resolved tolerance per cut so users can inspect. If a regression surfaces in the tester sweep, add an opt-out env var `IFCFAST_LEGACY_HALFSPACE_TOLERANCE=1` for one minor release.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: IfcMappedItem cache invariant (W14) — fix reveals that substrate consumers (clash, QTO, viewer) were relying on the wrong-but-consistent post-cut representation cache. Fixing it changes downstream fingerprint columns silently.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Pair W14 with W16's _CACHE_SCHEMA_VERSION bump and an explicit AGENTS.md note: 'representations.parquet now carries pre-cut shared meshes; instances.parquet fingerprints reflect post-cut topology'. Add an integration test asserting two instances of one mapped rep with different opening sets get different fingerprints but share the representation row. Communicate via release notes and worklog.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Polygon-bounded halfspace fix (W6) — the 0.7-ratio discriminator picks the wrong path for a file at the boundary. Either over-cut continues (current behavior) or the polygon-bounded path runs on a Revit-oversize cutter and produces incorrect partial cut.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Make the discriminator threshold configurable via the CsgTolerances policy struct (W3). Add a `polygon_bounded_strategy: {Auto, ForceUnbounded, ForceTight}` field for debugging. Tester-sweep gate before promoting default-on: Sannergata match must stay ≥97.4% AND ifcopenshell tight-bounded fixture (g) must produce correct volume.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Bus-factor concentration: i_overlay (W6, W9), bumpalo+rustc-hash (W13), eventual csgrs (W15) are all small-maintainer pure-Rust crates. The pipeline accumulates pre-1.0 deps faster than ecosystems mature.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Vendor-or-fork policy: each pre-1.0 dep gets a facade module so the dep boundary is one file. Quarterly audit of dep health (last release date, open issues, maintainer activity). For deps that go stale, vendor into the tree as part of next release (i_overlay is the most likely candidate; ~2.5k LOC absorbable). Manifold's bus-factor is the worst case we're already living with — none of these new deps are more fragile than manifold's status quo.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Pre-flight validator (W3+W11) is too strict — Tekla and ArchiCAD breps that today silently fall back and produce uncut-but-visible geometry now produce typed Unsupported(NonManifoldInput) and never reach manifold. The user perceives 'ifcfast got worse' even though the bug was always there.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Two-stage rollout: W3 ships the validator INFRA but does not gate manifold on it. W11 then attempts manifold first, runs validator only on failure to assign a typed reason for the Fallback. Net: today's silent-fallback files surface diagnosis without losing fallback geometry. After 1-2 releases, evaluate adding a `csg-strict` feature flag that gates manifold ON validator success.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Test fixture corpus (W5) takes longer than estimated — synthesizing 14 distinct edge-case IFC files with correct expected volumes is tedious and error-prone.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Lean on existing real files (Sannergata, Duplex_A, G55_RIB) for the hard cases; only synthesize the small fixtures (door-in-wall, mullion window, tilted wall). Use ifcopenshell-python to author the synthetics — that side of the workflow is mature. Cap initial corpus at fixtures (a)(b)(c)(g)(j)(l) and add the rest as separate PRs.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: AGENTS.md drift between releases — multiple PRs touch AGENTS.md and the contract surface accumulates without being audited.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Per project CLAUDE.md, every PR that adds/renames/removes agent-visible primitive must touch AGENTS.md. W16 is the consolidating PR for the foundation phase. Add a CI check (regex grep) that any PR touching mesh/cut_openings.rs OR mesh/boolean.rs OR lib.rs Outcome enum also touches AGENTS.md.
